channel.title: shuuino.party — reposts
channel.description: links, articles, and videos i find interesting

item.title: I Decompiled the White House's New App
item.description: The official White House Android app has a cookie/paywall bypass injector, tracks your GPS every 4.5 minutes, and loads JavaScript from some guy's GitHub Pages.

item.title: ▶ Video — youtube.com (mar 20, 2026)
item.description: A video shared on my repost. Click through to watch on YouTube.

item.title: Meta Lobbying and Other Findings
item.description: A documented collection of Meta's lobbying activity and policy influence — the kind of thing that doesn't make headlines but shapes the rules everyone has to live by.

item.title: Historic Chat Control Vote: MEPs Block Mass Scanning of Private Chats
item.description: The EU Parliament voted to reject untargeted surveillance of all private messages. A significant win for encrypted communication, though the fight is far from over.

item.title: California Law Requires Age Verification on All Operating Systems — Including Linux
item.description: A new California law mandates some form of age verification at account setup for all operating systems. The implications for open-source software and user privacy are significant.

item.title: Persona
item.description: A thoughtful post on identity, online personas, and the gap between who we present ourselves to be and who we actually are.

item.title: How Age Verification Systems Actually Work (and Fail)
item.description: A technical breakdown of what "age verification" really means in practice — and why every proposed implementation either doesn't work or creates a surveillance infrastructure far worse than the problem it claims to solve.

item.title: Hacking 35 Million+ Snaps — Follow-up Findings
item.description: The follow-up to the original bug report: further research uncovering the scale and implications of the Snapchat vulnerability.

item.title: EU Develops Independent Payment System to Reduce Reliance on Global Providers
item.description: The EU is building its own payment infrastructure to reduce dependence on US-based processors — part of a broader push for digital sovereignty.

item.title: I'm 15 and I Think I Found a Bug Affecting 10 Million+ Apps
item.description: A teenager discovers a significant security flaw in a widely-used service through methodical, independent research — a good reminder that age isn't a barrier to serious security work.
